name: Depth-Layered Canvas
description: A true z-axis spatial layout with foreground, midground, and background layers using CSS perspective and transform-style: preserve-3d. Layers stack along the depth axis with parallax scrolling and focal-plane blur effects. For immersive dashboards, data landscapes, and spatial storytelling where information lives at different visual depths.

three-plane.description: Classic three-plane depth layout with background environment, midground content, and foreground controls. Parallax ratio: background 0.3x, midground 0.7x, foreground 1.0x.

focal-spotlight.description: Single focal point in the foreground with all other content defocused in the background. DepthSlider lets users shift focus between layers. Useful for detail-on-demand data exploration.

flat-fallback.description: Flat stacked layout for reduced-motion or non-supporting browsers. All layers collapse to a vertical stack with no perspective transforms. Content ordering preserved: background, midground, foreground top-to-bottom.

slots.background-layer: Deepest visual plane. Ambient content: gradients, data visualizations, environment textures, or slow-moving decorative elements. Rendered at lowest z-index with strongest blur and slowest parallax rate (0.3x scroll speed).
slots.midground-layer: Primary content plane. Cards, panels, charts, and interactive widgets live here. Medium parallax rate (0.7x). This is where most user attention should land.
slots.foreground-layer: Closest visual plane. Navigation controls, floating action buttons, and contextual toolbars. Moves at 1:1 scroll speed with no blur. Always interactive.
slots.focal-point: A single highlighted element that draws depth-of-field focus. When active, surrounding layers receive graduated blur via BlurMask. Can be any content type: a card, chart, or media element.
slots.depth-controls: DepthSlider and layer visibility toggles. Lets users adjust parallax intensity, shift focal plane, or collapse to flat view. Should dock to an edge or float accessibly.
slots.parallax-track: The scroll or gesture track that drives parallax motion. Can be vertical scroll, horizontal drag, or pointer-follow. ParallaxContainer reads this track to translate layer positions.

accessibility.aria_label: Spatial content canvas with depth layers
announcements: Announce active focal layer when depth changes
Announce when transitioning between flat and spatial view
keyboard: Tab moves focus between interactive elements across all layers, foreground-first
Arrow Up/Down adjusts depth slider when focused
Escape collapses to flat fallback view
F key toggles focal-point spotlight mode
accessibility.reduce_motion: Collapse all layers to flat-fallback preset. Disable parallax transforms. Remove blur transitions. Preserve content order and interactivity.

responsive.mobile: Collapse to flat-fallback preset. Stack layers vertically. DepthSlider hidden. Focal-point renders inline without blur effect.
responsive.tablet: Reduce parallax intensity to 50%. Two visible planes (midground + foreground). Background becomes a static backdrop. DepthSlider available but simplified.

visual_brief: Three-plane spatial layout using CSS perspective and preserve-3d transforms. Background layer sits deepest with ambient gradients or data visualizations, rendered with blur and slow parallax. Midground layer holds the primary content cards and panels at normal focus. Foreground layer contains interactive controls, tooltips, and floating action elements at the closest depth. A depth slider control on the edge lets users adjust focal plane. Layers shift on scroll with different parallax rates. The flat-fallback preset collapses all layers to a standard stacked layout for reduced-motion preference.

composition.DepthLayeredCanvas: Container(d-section, perspective, preserve-3d, full) > [BackgroundLayer + MidgroundLayer + ForegroundLayer + DepthControls?]
